evals.prompt: Design the first three worlds of a new lean4game course on metric spaces for math undergraduates who know proofs weakly and know no Lean.
evals.expected_output: A course plan with explicit coverage and granularity, including onboarding, first definitions, proof moves, and a world graph.
expectations: The output separates macro mathematical coverage from micro proof-move coverage.
The output includes an explicit coverage map or equivalent tracking of introduction, practice, retrieval, integration, and transfer.
The output gives a world graph with distinct world archetypes rather than a flat list of theorems.
The output includes a novelty budget or equivalent granularity control.
The output identifies at least one misconception or notation hazard that deserves dedicated teaching.

evals.prompt: Given an existing lean4game repo modeled on a historical narrative course, propose one new pset world and one new lecture world without breaking house style.
evals.expected_output: A style-preserving expansion plan grounded in the repo’s existing rhythm, voice, coverage logic, and boss formula.
expectations: The output infers local house style before proposing new content.
The output distinguishes lecture-world and pset-world behavior.
The output explains how the new worlds fit the repo’s existing coverage and granularity.
The output updates dependencies or discusses dependency effects explicitly.
The output names specific regression risks created by inserting the new material.

evals.prompt: Audit a draft world containing 18 levels, three new tactics, and no pset follow-up.
evals.expected_output: A serious critique that flags world-size, granularity, novelty concentration, and missing transfer structure, with a split or redesign proposal.
expectations: The output flags the world as too large or at least questions its granularity strongly.
The output identifies novelty concentration as a problem.
The output comments on missing transfer / pset closure.
The output proposes a concrete split, merge, or restructure plan.
The output does not confuse compilation or completeness with pedagogical quality.

evals.prompt: Write a level introducing the definition of compactness for the first time, but do so at the right granularity for a lean4game audience.
evals.expected_output: A level plan or full level file that isolates the right dominant lesson, controls novelty, and explains why the chosen statement is the correct first-contact vehicle.
expectations: The output states the dominant lesson explicitly.
The output explains the novelty budget.
The output chooses a statement shape that simplifies surrounding burdens so compactness itself can be seen.
The output provides a hint strategy suited to first contact.
The output includes a conclusion or equivalent consolidation step.

evals.prompt: Perform a maintenance pass on a lean4game repo after users report missing tactic examples, missing familiar aliases, and a recently broken local setup.
evals.expected_output: A maintenance plan that treats the user reports as pedagogical evidence and addresses both technical and course-quality fallout.
expectations: The output treats missing examples and aliases as learner-facing quality problems, not cosmetic issues.
The output includes a plan for docs or discoverability improvements.
The output includes a plan for alias support or an explicit rationale if aliases should remain hidden.
The output includes local-run / toolchain maintenance steps.
The output includes a post-patch playtest plan focused on the affected learner path.
